industries.name: PVC & Vinyl Plastics
uses: Chlorine reacts with ethylene to form 1,2-dichloroethane (EDC), then cracked to vinyl chloride monomer (VCM) — the feedstock for polyvinyl chloride (PVC), the world's third-largest-volume plastic
Rigid PVC for pipes, fittings, window profiles (UPVC), guttering, electrical conduit, and vinyl siding — dominant material due to low cost, corrosion resistance, and fire retardance
Flexible PVC compounded with plasticizers (phthalates, DINP) for cable insulation, flooring (luxury vinyl tile), medical tubing, blood bags, IV bags, and upholstery
CPVC (chlorinated PVC) for hot water pipe systems and fire sprinkler systems — higher chlorine content gives improved heat resistance versus standard PVC
PVC production is the largest single use of chlorine globally — PVC contains 57% chlorine by weight, locking the element into long-lasting polymer products

companies.name: Shin-Etsu Chemical
companies.use: World's largest PVC producer. Operates integrated chlor-alkali, VCM, and PVC plants in Japan and the US (via Shintech). Supplies PVC resin to pipe, profile, and film manufacturers globally.

companies.name: Westlake Corporation
companies.use: Fully integrated US chlor-alkali, VCM, and PVC producer. Operates chlor-alkali plants in Louisiana and Texas feeding PVC resin plants. Major supplier to US pipe, window, and siding markets.

companies.name: Mexichem (Orbia)
companies.use: Largest PVC producer in the Americas outside the US. Fully integrated from salt through chlor-alkali to VCM and PVC resin. Supplies pipe, fittings, and specialty PVC compounds across Latin America and Europe.

companies.name: Inovyn (INEOS)
companies.use: Europe's largest PVC and chlor-alkali producer. Operates chlor-alkali, VCM, and PVC plants across UK, Belgium, Norway, and Germany. Supplies European pipe, profile, and flexible film markets.

industries.name: Water Treatment & Disinfection
uses: Chlorine gas (Cl₂) dosed directly into municipal water treatment plants for primary disinfection — kills bacteria, viruses, and protozoa; residual protects distribution network
Sodium hypochlorite (NaOCl, liquid bleach) generated from chlorine for use where gas handling is impractical — swimming pools, small water systems, and household disinfection
Chloramines (monochloramine) formed by reacting chlorine with ammonia — provide more stable residual in water distribution systems, reduce disinfection byproduct (trihalomethane) formation
Chlorine dioxide (ClO₂) for taste and odor control, biofilm removal, and primary disinfection in systems where trihalomethane formation is a regulatory concern
Wastewater effluent disinfection before discharge to receiving waters — chlorination followed by dechlorination with sodium bisulfite to protect aquatic life

companies.name: Veolia Water Technologies
companies.use: World's largest water and wastewater treatment operator. Manages municipal water systems serving hundreds of millions of people using chlorine-based disinfection. Operates on-site chlorine generation and hypochlorite dosing systems.

companies.name: SUEZ Water Technologies
companies.use: Major water utility operator and treatment technology company using chlorine, chloramines, and chlorine dioxide for municipal water disinfection across North America, Europe, and Asia-Pacific.

companies.name: Evoqua Water Technologies
companies.use: Leading supplier of on-site sodium hypochlorite generation systems (OSEC) and chlorine dioxide generators for municipalities, food processing, and industrial water treatment.

companies.name: Olin Corporation
companies.use: Largest US chlorine producer. Supplies chlorine gas in ton containers to municipal water utilities and liquid caustic soda as co-product. Also supplies sodium hypochlorite to commercial and industrial customers.

industries.name: Solvents, Refrigerants & Specialty Chemicals
uses: Chlorinated solvents: methylene chloride (DCM), trichloroethylene (TCE), and perchloroethylene (PCE, 'perc') used for metal degreasing, dry cleaning, and pharmaceutical synthesis — most under regulatory pressure due to toxicity and carcinogenicity
HFCs (hydrofluorocarbons) and HFOs — modern refrigerants replacing ozone-depleting CFCs — are manufactured via chlorine chemistry (HCl reacts with fluorine compounds)
HCFCs (HCFC-22 for air conditioning) still widely used in developing countries; being phased out under Montreal Protocol by 2030
Chlorine used to produce silicone precursors: Müller-Rochow reaction produces methylchlorosilanes from silicon and methyl chloride — backbone of all silicone polymers
Carbon tetrachloride (CCl₄) and chloroform (CHCl₃) as intermediates in pharmaceutical, agrochemical, and fluorochemical synthesis

companies.name: Honeywell International
companies.use: Pioneer of HFO (hydrofluoroolefin) refrigerants. Produces Solstice HFO-1234yf (automotive AC) and HFO-1234ze using chlorine-based synthesis routes. Also produces HCFC-22 and HFC-134a.

companies.name: Chemours (DuPont)
companies.use: Major refrigerant manufacturer producing Opteon HFO and Freon HFC refrigerants using chlorine feedstocks. Largest global supplier of fluoropolymers including PTFE (Teflon).

companies.name: Dow
companies.use: Produces chlorinated intermediates for silicone manufacturing (methyl chloride) and epoxy resins (epichlorohydrin). Major producer of chlorinated solvents and chlorine-derived specialty chemicals.

companies.name: Solvay
companies.use: Major European producer of chlorinated solvents, hydrogen chloride, and chlorine-derived specialty chemicals for pharmaceutical, agrochemical, and fluorine chemistry applications.

industries.name: Agrochemicals & Pharmaceuticals
uses: Chlorine is a key building block in agrochemical synthesis — approximately 40% of all pesticides and herbicides contain at least one chlorine atom in their structure
Herbicides: atrazine (Cl), 2,4-D (2 Cl), glyphosate synthesis uses HCl, acetochlor (Cl) — dominant row crop weed control chemistries in corn, soy, and wheat
Fungicides: chlorothalonil, propiconazole, tebuconazole — broad-spectrum foliar fungicides for cereal, fruit, and vegetable crops
Insecticides: chlorpyrifos, permethrin, chlorfenapyr — widely used in crop protection and vector control (malaria mosquitoes)
Pharmaceuticals: chlorine present in ibuprofen synthesis, sertraline (Zoloft), clonazepam, amoxicillin — approximately 30% of active pharmaceutical ingredients contain chlorine

companies.name: Bayer Crop Science
companies.use: World's largest agrochemical company (following Monsanto acquisition). Produces chlorine-containing herbicides, fungicides, and insecticides including glufosinate and propiconazole for global crop protection.

companies.name: Syngenta (ChemChina)
companies.use: Major agrochemical producer making chlorine-containing herbicides (atrazine, metolachlor) and fungicides (azoxystrobin, propiconazole, difenoconazole) for global crop protection markets.

companies.name: BASF Agricultural Solutions
companies.use: Major producer of chlorine-containing fungicides (boscalid, metconazole), herbicides, and insecticides. Integrates chlorine chemistry from its Verbund chemical production network into agrochemical synthesis.

companies.name: Pfizer
companies.use: Major pharmaceutical company using chlorinated intermediates in active pharmaceutical ingredient synthesis. Azithromycin, sertraline, and numerous other blockbuster drugs require chlorine-based synthesis chemistry.
